full_title: ASCE/SEI 7-22 Minimum Design Loads and Associated Criteria for Buildings and Other Structures
sources: Meca Enterprises - Wind Loads on Solid Signs (mecaenterprises.com)
Meca Enterprises - Wind Loads on Freestanding Walls (mecaenterprises.com)
Meca Enterprises - ASCE 7-22 Wind Load Changes (mecaenterprises.com)
SkyCiv Engineering - ASCE 7 Wind Load Calculations (skyciv.com)
CED Engineering - S02-048 Calculating Wind Loads Using ASCE 7-22 (cedengineering.com)
Bentley STAAD.Pro - ASCE 7-22 Wind Load Generation (docs.bentley.com)
The StructEd - How Does Site Elevation Influence Wind Loads (structed.org)
safety_notice: SAFETY-CRITICAL DATA: These values are used in structural calculations subject to PE stamp. All values must be verified against the official ASCE 7-22 standard before use in production calculations. When in doubt, use the more conservative (higher) value.

Kz_table_26_10_1.description: Velocity Pressure Exposure Coefficients, Kh and Kz (Table 26.10-1)
notes: For z <= 15 ft, use Kz value for 15 ft height
For z >= zg, Kz = 2.01*(zg/zg)^(2/alpha) = 2.01
Linear interpolation is permitted for intermediate heights

ASCE 7-22 made minor reductions to some Exposure B values (z >= 40ft) and Exposure C values (z >= 140ft) based on improved research. The values here are the ASCE 7-16 baseline which is conservative (slightly higher). See Kz_7_22_notes for details.
Kz_table_26_10_1.formula_note: z = height above ground (ft), zg and alpha from Table 26.11-1

Kz_7_22_notes.description: ASCE 7-22 made minor reductions to Exposure B (z >= 40ft) and Exposure C (z >= 140ft). Using ASCE 7-16 values is conservative (produces slightly higher loads). If jurisdiction requires exact ASCE 7-22 values, consult the standard directly.

notes: ASCE 7-22 Change: Kd removed from the velocity pressure equation (qz) and placed into individual wind pressure/force equations
For MWFRS design using directional procedure: Kd per this table
For non-directional procedure: Kd = 1.0

buildings_MWFRS.description: Buildings - Main Wind Force Resisting System

chimneys_tanks_similar.description: Chimneys, Tanks, and Similar Structures - Square

chimneys_tanks_hex_round.description: Chimneys, Tanks, and Similar Structures - Hexagonal, Round

solid_freestanding_walls_signs.description: Solid Freestanding Walls and Solid Freestanding Signs

trussed_towers_all_other.description: Trussed Towers - All Other Cross Sections

notes: New in ASCE 7-22 (not in ASCE 7-16)
Accounts for reduced air density at higher elevations
It is permitted to use Ke = 1.0 for all elevations (conservative)
Interpolation is permitted for intermediate elevations
Ke_table_26_9_1.formula: Ke = e^(-0.0000362 * zg_elev)  [approximate, zg_elev in feet above sea level]
Ke_table_26_9_1.formula_note: This formula is approximate; use table values for design. For zg_elev > 6000 ft, use the formula or consult the standard.

Cf_figure_29_3_1.description: Force Coefficients, Cf, for Solid Freestanding Walls and Solid Freestanding Signs (Figure 29.3-1)
notes: Cf depends on B/s ratio (width-to-height of sign) and s/h ratio (sign height / mounting height to top)
B = horizontal dimension of sign (ft)
s = vertical dimension of sign (ft)
h = height to top of sign above ground (ft)
Three loading cases: A, B, and C
Cases A and B must always be checked
Case C only applies when B/s >= 2

case_A.description: Resultant acts normal to face of sign through geometric center
case_A.application: Governs panel framing in double-pole signs

values.interpolation_note: Linear interpolation is permitted for intermediate B/s and s/h values

case_B.description: Resultant acts normal to face of sign at 0.2*B eccentricity from geometric center (toward windward edge)
case_B.application: Governs torsion on single-pole signs, individual pole loads on double-pole signs
case_B.Cf_values: Same Cf values as Case A, but applied at the eccentric location
case_B.note: The Cf magnitude is the same as Case A. The difference is the point of application (eccentric by 0.2*B). This creates torsional moment = F * 0.2*B.

case_C.description: Non-uniform load distribution for long signs/walls (B/s >= 2). Force applied normal to face with varying Cf along the width.
case_C.application: Governs for long freestanding walls and wide signs

zones.description: Sign width divided into zones of height 's', each with different Cf. Zone boundaries measured from windward free end.

case_C.interpolation_note: Linear interpolation is permitted for intermediate B/s values. For B/s > 45, use B/s = 45 values.

Kzt_note.note: Kzt = 1.0 for flat terrain (no hills, ridges, or escarpments). For sites near topographic features, Kzt must be calculated per Section 26.8. Most sign installations use Kzt = 1.0.

gust_factor.note: G = 0.85 for rigid structures (natural frequency >= 1 Hz). Most signs are rigid structures. For flexible structures, G must be calculated per Section 26.11.5.

velocity_pressure.units: qz in psf, V in mph
velocity_pressure.note: ASCE 7-22 CHANGE: Kd is NO LONGER in the velocity pressure equation. It is now applied in individual pressure/force equations.
velocity_pressure.asce_7_16_equation: qz = 0.00256 * Kz * Kzt * Kd * V^2 (Kd was in this equation in ASCE 7-16)

design_wind_force_signs.equation: F = qz * Kd * G * Cf * As
design_wind_force_signs.units: F in lbf, qz in psf, As in sq ft

alternative_combined_form.equation: F = 0.00256 * Kz * Kzt * Ke * V^2 * Kd * G * Cf * As
alternative_combined_form.note: Combined form showing all factors. This produces the same result whether using ASCE 7-16 or 7-22 equation arrangements.

step_1_velocity_pressure.note: Note: qz does NOT include Kd in ASCE 7-22

step_2_force_coefficient.interpolation: Interpolate between s/h=0.25 and s/h=0.50 at B/s=2.0
step_2_force_coefficient.note: From Figure 29.3-1 Case A: at B/s=2.0, s/h=0.25 gives Cf=1.70 and s/h=0.50 gives Cf=1.70, so Cf=1.70

step_3_design_force_case_A.equation: F = qz * Kd * G * Cf * As
step_3_design_force_case_A.note: This is the uniform load case (Case A) - force acts at geometric center

step_3_alt_combined.equation: F = 0.00256 * Kz * Kzt * Ke * V^2 * Kd * G * Cf * As

step_4_case_B_torsion.description: Case B: Same force as Case A but applied at 0.2*B eccentricity

step_5_case_C_check.note: Case C gives higher total force than Case A for B/s >= 2. Check all three cases.

cross_check_qz_with_Kd_in_qz.description: Cross-check: If using the older ASCE 7-16 form with Kd in qz
cross_check_qz_with_Kd_in_qz.equation: qz = 0.00256 * Kz * Kzt * Kd * Ke * V^2
cross_check_qz_with_Kd_in_qz.note: This matches the task expected value of ~24.5 psf. The ASCE 7-16 form embeds Kd in qz.

B.description: Urban and suburban areas, wooded areas, or other terrain with numerous closely spaced obstructions having the size of single-family dwellings or larger
B.typical_use: Most sign installations in developed areas

C.description: Open terrain with scattered obstructions having heights generally less than 30 ft. Includes flat open country, grasslands, and all water surfaces in hurricane-prone regions
C.typical_use: Signs along highways, in rural areas, or near airports

D.description: Flat, unobstructed areas and water surfaces outside hurricane-prone regions. Includes smooth mud flats, salt flats, and unbroken ice
D.typical_use: Rare for sign installations; coastal areas outside hurricane zones

risk_categories_table_1_5_1.description: Risk Category of Buildings and Other Structures (Table 1.5-1)
notes: ASCE 7-10 and later (including 7-22) ELIMINATED the wind importance factor Iw
Instead of Iw, separate wind speed maps are provided for each Risk Category
Each map uses a different mean recurrence interval (MRI)
Signs are typically Risk Category I or II depending on occupancy/exposure

I.description: Buildings and other structures that represent a low risk to human life in the event of failure
I.examples: Agricultural facilities, minor storage facilities, temporary facilities
I.sign_application: Signs in isolated locations with minimal public exposure

II.description: All buildings and other structures except those listed in Risk Categories I, III, and IV
II.examples: Most commercial buildings, residential buildings, standard occupancy structures

III.description: Buildings and other structures, the failure of which could pose a substantial risk to human life, or whose failure could cause a substantial economic impact or mass disruption of day-to-day civilian life
III.examples: Buildings with >300 occupants, schools (>250), healthcare without surgery, jails, power stations
III.sign_application: Signs on or adjacent to Risk Category III buildings

IV.description: Buildings and other structures designated as essential facilities
IV.examples: Hospitals, fire/rescue/police stations, emergency shelters, air traffic control towers
IV.sign_application: Signs on essential facilities (rare for typical sign work)

risk_categories_table_1_5_1.importance_factor_note: ASCE 7-22 does NOT use wind importance factors. The wind speed maps themselves are calibrated to each Risk Category's MRI. Do not multiply by Iw.

basic_wind_speed_reference.description: Basic Wind Speed V Reference (Section 26.5, Figures 26.5-1 through 26.5-4)
notes: ASCE 7-22 provides 4 separate wind speed maps, one per Risk Category
Wind speeds are 3-second gust speeds at 33 ft (10 m) above ground in Exposure C
Site-specific speeds must be obtained from ASCE 7 Hazard Tool (asce7hazardtool.online) or wind speed maps
The values below are APPROXIMATE TYPICAL ranges for non-hurricane regions of the continental US

hurricane_prone_regions.atlantic_gulf_coast: 130-180 mph (Risk Cat II), varies significantly by location
hurricane_prone_regions.note: Hurricane-prone coastal areas have significantly higher design wind speeds. Always use site-specific lookup.

basic_wind_speed_reference.critical_note: NEVER estimate wind speed. Always use the ASCE 7 Hazard Tool or jurisdictional requirements for the specific project location.

sign_calculation_workflow.step_1: Determine basic wind speed V from ASCE 7-22 wind speed maps (Risk Category dependent)
sign_calculation_workflow.step_2: Determine Exposure Category (B, C, or D) based on surrounding terrain
sign_calculation_workflow.step_3: Determine height z for Kz lookup (centroid of sign above ground, or evaluate at multiple heights)
sign_calculation_workflow.step_5: Determine Kzt (1.0 for flat terrain, or calculate per Section 26.8)
sign_calculation_workflow.step_6: Determine Ke from Table 26.9-1 (or use 1.0 conservatively)
sign_calculation_workflow.step_7: Calculate qz = 0.00256 * Kz * Kzt * Ke * V^2
sign_calculation_workflow.step_8: Look up Kd from Table 26.6-1 (0.85 for signs)
sign_calculation_workflow.step_10: Compute B/s and s/h ratios for the sign
sign_calculation_workflow.step_11: Look up Cf from Figure 29.3-1 for Cases A, B, and C (if applicable)
sign_calculation_workflow.step_12: Calculate F = qz * Kd * G * Cf * As for each case
sign_calculation_workflow.step_13: Design for the governing (worst-case) loading condition
